features.description: Establish the site development workflow contract, including the repository role boundary, workflow state files, and the verification entrypoint.
Add repository contract tests that protect the Obsidian-to-Hugo publishing boundary and the Selected summary CSS regression.
Document the workflow-driven requirement, planning, coding, and evaluation loop for future site changes.
Update the Projects page Gentle Memories entry to show community review approval and link to the public Obsidian review page.
Refine article code block rendering with GitHub-style syntax highlighting, readable typography, hidden text labels, and inline-code-matched block backgrounds.
Refresh the public About and Resume pages with resume-informed professional copy, privacy-safe public profile content, and code-block-aligned terminal styling.
Add giscus-powered article comments backed by GitHub Discussions, with reactions enabled and post-page rendering covered by tests.
Add site-hosted custom giscus themes so the comment iframe background matches the site's light and dark palettes.
Add a lightweight selection-to-comment flow that copies selected article text as a Markdown quote and scrolls readers to giscus comments.
Add an English and Simplified Chinese Hugo site shell with preserved English URLs, a translation-aware language switch, localized interface copy, and bilingual build coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Existing English pages remain at their current root-relative URLs while the Chinese site is generated under /zh/.
Every rendered page exposes an English/Chinese language switch that targets the paired translation when available and the target-language homepage otherwise.
Homepage, lists, posts, taxonomies, series navigation, table of contents, comments, dates, counts, and empty states use localized interface strings.
Translated pages and language homepages emit correct alternate-language metadata without client-side redirects.
Automated tests and the production Hugo build verify both language outputs without modifying generated article content.
